ucs.title: [LLM] Akira locker pre-encryption: PowerShell WMI shadow-copy wipe correlated with akira_readme.txt / .akira artefacts on the same host
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Akira / Howling Scorpius / GOLD SAHARA / PUNK SPIDER pre-encryption chain — Akira's locker (and the Megazord/Akira_v2 builds) deletes Volume Shadow Copies via a PowerShell WMI one-liner (`Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy | Remove-WmiObject`) immediately before dropping `akira_readme.txt` and renaming victim files to `.akira` / `.powerranges`. The dual-event correlation in a tight window on the same DeviceId is what differentiates Akira from generic ransomware that uses vssadmin alone.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as shadow_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" OR Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe") (Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*" OR Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*" OR Processes.process="*delete*shadows*" OR Processes.process="*Remove-WmiObject*Win32_Shadowcopy*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as shadow_first lastTime as shadow_last | join type=inner dest [ | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as artifact_first max(_time) as artifact_last values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="akira_readme.txt" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.akira" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.powerranges") by Filesystem.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` ] | where artifact_first >= shadow_first AND artifact_first <= shadow_last + 7200 | table dest user shadow_first artifact_first shadow_cmd files paths
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 7200;  // 2h between VSS wipe and locker artefact
let ShadowWipe = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","vssadmin.exe","cmd.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject","shadowcopy delete","delete shadows","shadows /all /quiet","vssadmin delete shadows")
| project ShadowTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ShadowProc = FileName, ShadowCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ShadowParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
let AkiraArtefacts = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
| where FileName =~ "akira_readme.txt"
   or FileName endswith ".akira"
   or FileName endswith ".powerranges"
   or FileName endswith ".akiranew"
| project ArtefactTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
          ArtefactFile = FileName, ArtefactPath = FolderPath,
          DropperProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DropperSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256;
ShadowWipe
| join kind=inner AkiraArtefacts on DeviceId
| where ArtefactTime between (ShadowTime .. ShadowTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', ArtefactTime, ShadowTime)
| summarize ShadowTime = min(ShadowTime),
            FirstArtefact = min(ArtefactTime),
            LastArtefact = max(ArtefactTime),
            ArtefactCount = dcount(ArtefactPath),
            SampleArtefacts = make_set(ArtefactFile, 10),
            SampleArtefactPaths = make_set(ArtefactPath, 10),
            DropperProcs = make_set(DropperProc, 5),
            DropperHashes = make_set(DropperSHA256, 5),
            ShadowCmd = any(ShadowCmd),
            ShadowProc = any(ShadowProc),
            DelaySec = min(DelaySec)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName
| order by ShadowTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 7200;
let ShadowWipe = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any ("Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject","shadowcopy delete","delete shadows","shadows /all /quiet","vssadmin delete shadows")
| project ShadowTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ShadowProc = NewProcessName, ShadowCmd = CommandLine;
let AkiraArtefacts = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4663                                  // object-access write
| where ObjectName endswith "\\akira_readme.txt"
     or ObjectName endswith ".akira"
     or ObjectName endswith ".powerranges"
     or ObjectName endswith ".akiranew"
| project ArtefactTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
          ArtefactPath = ObjectName, ProcessName;
ShadowWipe
| join kind=inner AkiraArtefacts on Computer
| where ArtefactTime between (ShadowTime .. ShadowTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', ArtefactTime, ShadowTime)
| summarize FirstArtefact = min(ArtefactTime),
            LastArtefact = max(ArtefactTime),
            ArtefactCount = dcount(ArtefactPath),
            SampleArtefacts = make_set(ArtefactPath, 10),
            ShadowCmd = any(ShadowCmd),
            DelaySec = min(DelaySec)
            by ShadowTime, Computer, Account
| order by ShadowTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Akira Ransomware Pre-Encryption Shadow Copy Wipe via PowerShell WMI
id: 7d9c2a4e-1f2a-4b8d-9c44-akira-vss-001
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the signature PowerShell WMI one-liner used by the Akira / Howling Scorpius / PUNK SPIDER / GOLD SAHARA
  RaaS operation immediately before invocation of their Rust-based locker. Akira affiliates have used this
  exact command form across W.10/11/Server intrusions tracked by CISA AA24-109A and Mandiant since 2023.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-109a
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1024/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1047
  - actor.akira
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'Win32_Shadowcopy'
      - 'Remove-WmiObject'
  selection_cmd_alt:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy'
      - 'gwmi Win32_Shadowcopy'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_proc and (selection_cmd or selection_cmd_alt) and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Backup-product cleanup tasks (rare in user-context PowerShell — usually System or scheduled task)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Akira's locker chain has been documented by CISA AA24-109A, Sophos X-Ops, and Mandiant as following an extremely specific sequence: a PowerShell-hosted WMI shadow-copy deletion (`Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy | Remove-WmiObject` — chosen over plain `vssadmin` so the call survives basic AV string-matching) followed within minutes by drop of `akira_readme.txt` and file extension rename to `.akira`
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Akira post-VPN-compromise toolkit staging: AdFind + SoftPerfect/Advanced IP Scanner + AnyDesk/RustDesk + Rclone within 24h on one host
ucs.description: Hunts Akira's documented hands-on-keyboard playbook immediately after VPN-gateway compromise (Cisco AnyConnect without MFA per CVE-2023-20269, SonicWall SSL VPN, plus stolen ESXi creds) — the actor stages a tight tool stack (AdFind for domain recon, SoftPerfect/Advanced IP Scanner for sweep, AnyDesk or RustDesk for persistent remote-access C2, Rclone/WinSCP for exfil to MEGA before encryption). Catching ≥2 of these tool families on one host inside 24h fingerprints Akira/PUNK SPIDER/Howling Scorpius tradecraft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process_name) as tools values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("adfind.exe","advanced_ip_scanner.exe","advanced_port_scanner.exe","netscan.exe","netscan64.exe") OR Processes.process_name IN ("anydesk.exe","rustdesk.exe","cloudflared.exe","ngrok.exe","radmin.exe") OR Processes.process_name IN ("rclone.exe","winscp.exe","filezilla.exe","megasync.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*-f \"name=*\"*","*-listdcmodes*","*trustdmp*","*dcmodes*","*objectcategory=computer*")) by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval has_discovery=if(match(tools,"adfind.exe|advanced_ip_scanner.exe|advanced_port_scanner.exe|netscan(64)?.exe"),1,0), has_rat=if(match(tools,"anydesk.exe|rustdesk.exe|cloudflared.exe|ngrok.exe|radmin.exe"),1,0), has_exfil=if(match(tools,"rclone.exe|winscp.exe|filezilla.exe|megasync.exe"),1,0), tool_families=has_discovery+has_rat+has_exfil, duration_h=round((lastSeen-firstSeen)/3600,2) | where tool_families>=2 AND duration_h<=24 | table dest users tools cmdlines firstSeen lastSeen duration_h tool_families | sort - tool_families - lastSeen
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let MaxStagingHours = 24h;
let DiscoveryTools = dynamic(["adfind.exe","advanced_ip_scanner.exe","advanced_port_scanner.exe","netscan.exe","netscan64.exe","softperfect network scanner.exe"]);
let RemoteAccessTools = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","rustdesk.exe","cloudflared.exe","ngrok.exe","radmin.exe","radminviewer.exe"]);
let ExfilTools = dynamic(["rclone.exe","winscp.exe","filezilla.exe","megasync.exe","megacmdserver.exe"]);
let AdFindArgs = dynamic(["-f \"name=","trustdmp","-listdcmodes","objectcategory=computer","objectcategory=person","objectcategory=organizationalunit"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (DiscoveryTools)
    or FileName in~ (RemoteAccessTools)
    or FileName in~ (ExfilTools)
    or (FileName =~ "adfind.exe" or ProcessCommandLine has_any (AdFindArgs))
| extend ToolFamily = case(
    FileName in~ (DiscoveryTools) or ProcessCommandLine has_any (AdFindArgs), "Discovery",
    FileName in~ (RemoteAccessTools), "RemoteAccess",
    FileName in~ (ExfilTools), "Exfil",
    "Other")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            ToolFamilies = make_set(ToolFamily),
            Tools = make_set(FileName, 20),
            CmdSamples = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 20),
            Users = make_set(AccountName, 10),
            ParentProcs = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            RemoteSessionFlag = max(tolong(IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession))
            by DeviceId, DeviceName
| extend DurationHrs = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen) / 60.0
| extend FamilyCount = array_length(ToolFamilies)
| where FamilyCount >= 2                          // at least 2 of {Discovery,RemoteAccess,Exfil}
| where DurationHrs <= 24                          // tight hands-on-keyboard window
| order by FamilyCount desc, LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let DiscoveryBins = dynamic(["adfind.exe","advanced_ip_scanner.exe","advanced_port_scanner.exe","netscan.exe","netscan64.exe"]);
let RemoteAccessBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","rustdesk.exe","cloudflared.exe","ngrok.exe","radmin.exe"]);
let ExfilBins = dynamic(["rclone.exe","winscp.exe","filezilla.exe","megasync.exe","megacmdserver.exe"]);
let AdFindArgs = dynamic(["-f \"name=","trustdmp","-listdcmodes","objectcategory=computer","objectcategory=organizationalunit"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend ExeName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ExeName in (DiscoveryBins)
    or ExeName in (RemoteAccessBins)
    or ExeName in (ExfilBins)
    or (ExeName == "adfind.exe" or CommandLine has_any (AdFindArgs))
| extend ToolFamily = case(
    ExeName in (DiscoveryBins) or CommandLine has_any (AdFindArgs), "Discovery",
    ExeName in (RemoteAccessBins), "RemoteAccess",
    ExeName in (ExfilBins), "Exfil",
    "Other")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            ToolFamilies = make_set(ToolFamily),
            Tools = make_set(ExeName, 20),
            CmdSamples = make_set(CommandLine, 20),
            Users = make_set(Account, 10)
            by Computer
| extend FamilyCount = array_length(ToolFamilies),
         DurationHrs = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen) / 60.0
| where FamilyCount >= 2 and DurationHrs <= 24
| order by FamilyCount desc, LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: CISA AA24-109A, Sophos X-Ops, Mandiant (UNC4393 overlap), and Palo Alto Unit 42 (Howling Scorpius) all converge on a remarkably stable Akira/PUNK SPIDER playbook on freshly compromised hosts: drop AdFind with `-f "name=*"` / `trustdmp` / `-listdcmodes` (signature AdFind invocations Akira uses verbatim across cases), launch SoftPerfect Network Scanner or Advanced IP Scanner for subnet sweep, instal
